childhood.familyBackground: Born April 22, 1985, to a Jewish American family. Mother Connie Gibstine is a dermatologist. Father Jerry Altman was a real estate broker. Eldest of four siblings: brothers Max and Jack, and sister Ann. Family relocated to Clayton, Missouri in 1989.
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 1985년 4월 22일 유대계 미국인 가정에서 태어남. 어머니 Connie Gibstine은 피부과 의사. 아버지 Jerry Altman은 부동산 중개인. 형 Max와 Jack, 누나 Ann과 함께 4명의 형제 중 맏형. 1989년 미주리주 Clayton으로 이전.
childhood.education: Attended John Burroughs School, a private institution in Ladue, Missouri. Studied computer science at Stanford University for two years before dropping out in 2005 without completing bachelor's degree. Later stated he learned more from playing poker with classmates than attending lectures.
childhood.educationKo: 미주리주 Ladue의 사립 기관 John Burroughs School 재학. 2005년 학사 학위를 취득하지 않고 중퇴하기 전까지 Stanford University에서 컴퓨터과학 2년 전공. 나중에 강의보다 동료와 포커를 하며 배운 것이 더 많다고 언급.
childhood.earlyLife: Received first computer (Apple Macintosh) at age 8 and began learning to code and disassembling computer hardware. Vegetarian since childhood. Close-knit family that ate dinner together nightly and bonded through games like table tennis, billiards, and charades. Early passion for technology and entrepreneurship.
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 8세에 첫 컴퓨터(Apple Macintosh)를 받고 코딩과 컴퓨터 하드웨어 분해를 배우기 시작. 어린 시절부터 채식주의자. 매일 밤 저녁을 함께하고 탁구, 빌리아드, 생각놀이 같은 게임으로 유대를 다진 긴밀한 가족. 기술과 기업가 정신에 대한 초기 열정.
childhood.source: Wikipedia, Britannica Money, Interesting Engineering, Medium, Press.farm

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded Loopt at age 19 with Nick Sivo and Alok Deshpande. Dropped out of Stanford University after two years to focus on the startup. Loopt was one of the earliest location-based social networking mobile applications.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 19세 때 Nick Sivo와 Alok Deshpande와 함께 Loopt 공동 창립. Stanford University에서 2년 후 중퇴하여 스타트업에 집중. Loopt는 초기 위치 기반 소셜 네트워킹 모바일 애플리케이션 중 하나

careerTimeline.event: Loopt received early funding and partnerships with wireless carriers including Sprint. Application began gaining initial traction in location-based social networking space.
careerTimeline.eventKo: Loopt이 Sprint를 포함한 무선 통신사와의 초기 자금 조달 및 파트너십 취득. 애플리케이션이 위치 기반 소셜 네트워킹 공간에서 초기 추진력 시작

careerTimeline.event: Loopt acquired by Green Dot Corporation for $43.4 million. Despite not achieving unicorn status, the acquisition provided Altman with substantial capital for future ventures.
careerTimeline.eventKo: Loopt이 Green Dot Corporation에 4,340만 달러에 인수됨. 유니콘 지위를 달성하지 못했음에도 불구하고 인수는 Altman에게 향후 사업을 위한 상당한 자본 제공

careerTimeline.event: Became President of Y Combinator, replacing co-founder Paul Graham. Took over leadership of the world's most prestigious startup accelerator at age 29.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 공동 창립자 Paul Graham을 대신하여 Y Combinator의 대표 취임. 29세의 나이에 세계에서 가장 저명한 스타트업 액셀러레이터의 리더십 인수

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded Hydrazine Capital (later renamed Apollo Projects) as a venture fund with backing from Peter Thiel. Made early investments in companies like Stripe, Airbnb, Reddit, and DoorDash.
careerTimeline.eventKo: Peter Thiel의 지원을 받아 벤처 펀드인 Hydrazine Capital(나중에 Apollo Projects로 개명) 공동 창립. Stripe, Airbnb, Reddit, DoorDash 같은 회사에 초기 투자 실행

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded OpenAI as a nonprofit organization dedicated to ensuring artificial general intelligence benefits humanity. Served as Chairman during early phase.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 인공지능이 인류에게 이로움을 주도록 보장하는 것에 전념하는 비영리 조직 OpenAI 공동 창립. 초기 단계에서 회장 역임

careerTimeline.event: Stepped down as President of Y Combinator after five years of leadership. Under his presidency, Y Combinator expanded to help approximately 1,900 companies including Airbnb, Instacart, DoorDash, Reddit, and Twitch.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 5년의 리더십 후 Y Combinator의 대표 직에서 물러남. 그의 회장 재직 중 Y Combinator는 Airbnb, Instacart, DoorDash, Reddit, Twitch를 포함한 약 1,900개의 회사를 지원하도록 확대

careerTimeline.event: Became CEO of OpenAI. Created OpenAI's for-profit subsidiary controlled by the nonprofit board to secure funding for large-scale AI research and development.
careerTimeline.eventKo: OpenAI의 CEO가 됨. 대규모 AI 연구 개발 자금을 확보하기 위해 비영리 이사회가 통제하는 OpenAI의 영리 자회사 창립

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded AltC Acquisition Corp (SPAC) with investment banker Michael Klein. Served as CEO of the special-purpose acquisition company.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 투자 은행가 Michael Klein과 함께 AltC Acquisition Corp(SPAC) 공동 창립. 특수 목적 인수 회사의 CEO 역임

careerTimeline.event: OpenAI launched ChatGPT in November 2022, which rapidly became the fastest-growing consumer application in history, reaching 100 million users in 2 months.
careerTimeline.eventKo: OpenAI가 2022년 11월 ChatGPT를 출시했으며, 빠르게 역사상 가장 빠르게 성장하는 소비자 애플리케이션이 되어 2개월 만에 1억 명의 사용자 달성

careerTimeline.event: November 17: Removed as OpenAI CEO by board of directors. November 21: Reinstated as CEO after massive employee backlash. Dramatic five-day boardroom coup that shook the AI industry.
careerTimeline.eventKo: 11월 17일: 이사회에 의해 OpenAI CEO 직에서 제거됨. 11월 21일: 대규모 직원 반발 후 CEO 직에 복직됨. AI 산업을 뒤흔든 극적인 5일의 이사회 쿠데타

careerTimeline.event: Oklo Inc. (nuclear energy company Altman backed through his SPAC) completed merger and became public company. Altman became increasingly focused on AI safety and nuclear energy as solutions to existential risks.
careerTimeline.eventKo: Oklo Inc.(Altman이 SPAC을 통해 지원한 핵에너지 회사)가 합병을 완료하고 상장 기업이 됨. Altman은 AI 안전과 핵 에너지에 점점 더 집중하여 실존적 위험에 대한 해결책으로 활용

failures.description: Loopt struggled to achieve sustainable user growth despite initial partnerships with major wireless carriers like Sprint. Product failed to gain mass adoption and public enthusiasm for constant location-sharing was overestimated.
failures.descriptionKo: Loopt은 Sprint 같은 주요 무선 통신사와의 초기 파트너십에도 불구하고 지속 가능한 사용자 성장을 달성하기 위해 고군분투. 제품이 대중 채택을 달성하지 못했으며 지속적인 위치 공유에 대한 공개적 열정이 과대평가됨.
failures.lesson: Validated learning about product-market fit challenges. Early growth and partnerships can mask weak user engagement fundamentals. The experience taught humility about predicting consumer behavior and the importance of core engagement metrics over vanity metrics.
failures.lessonKo: 제품-시장 부합 과제에 대한 학습 검증. 초기 성장과 파트너십은 약한 사용자 참여 기초를 숨길 수 있음. 이 경험은 소비자 행동 예측에 대한 겸손함과 허상 지표보다 핵심 참여 지표의 중요성을 배움.
failures.source: Press.farm, Failure Before Success, Mixergy, CNBC, Dynamic Business

failures.description: Steve Jobs told Altman he thought Loopt's product 'wasn't very good,' delivering a crushing emotional blow that made Altman question everything about the venture.
failures.descriptionKo: Steve Jobs가 Altman에게 Loopt의 제품이 '별로 좋지 않다'고 생각한다고 말하여 Altman이 사업의 모든 것을 의문하게 만드는 충격적인 정서적 타격을 입힘.
failures.lesson: Personal resilience and the ability to process critical feedback from industry leaders is essential. Altman later shared that he wished someone had told him that others don't think about your failures as much as you do, and that recovery is possible.
failures.lessonKo: 개인적 회복력과 산업 리더들의 비판적 피드백을 처리하는 능력이 필수적. Altman은 나중에 누군가 자신의 실패에 대해 다른 사람들이 당신만큼 많이 생각하지 않으며 회복이 가능하다고 말해주었으면 좋았을 것 같다고 공유.

failures.description: Removed as CEO of OpenAI on November 17, 2023, by the board citing lack of confidence in his leadership and transparency. Cited as not being 'consistently candid in his communications with the board.'
failures.descriptionKo: 2023년 11월 17일 이사회에 의해 OpenAI CEO 직에서 제거, 리더십과 투명성에 대한 신뢰 부족 인용. 이사회와의 의사소통에서 '지속적으로 솔직하지 못했다'고 인용됨.
failures.lesson: Even transformational leaders face institutional governance challenges. Demonstrated the importance of board alignment and transparent communication in high-stakes organizations. Also revealed the power of employee and investor support in leadership crises.
failures.lessonKo: 변혁적 리더도 제도적 지배 구조 과제에 직면. 고위험 조직에서 이사회 조정과 투명한 의사소통의 중요성을 입증. 또한 리더십 위기에서 직원과 투자자 지원의 힘을 드러냄.

quotes.text: Self-belief is immensely powerful. The most successful people I know believe in themselves, almost to the point of delusion.
quotes.textKo: 자신감은 엄청나게 강력하다. 내가 아는 가장 성공한 사람들은 자신을 거의 망상 수준까지 믿는다.
quotes.context: On the psychological foundation of entrepreneurial success and the role of conviction in achieving ambitious goals.
quotes.contextKo: 기업가적 성공의 심리적 기초와 야심찬 목표 달성에서의 확신의 역할에 대해

quotes.text: AI will probably most likely lead to the end of the world, but in the meantime, there'll be great companies.
quotes.textKo: AI는 아마도 세상의 종말로 이어질 것 같지만, 그때까지는 훌륭한 회사들이 있을 것이다.
quotes.context: On AI's dual nature as both tremendous opportunity and existential risk. Reflects his balanced but candid perspective on AI's trajectory.
quotes.contextKo: 엄청난 기회와 실존적 위험 모두로서의 AI의 이중 성질에 대해. AI의 궤적에 대한 균형잡힌 하지만 솔직한 관점을 반영

quotes.text: Mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority alongside other societal-scale risks such as pandemics and nuclear war.
quotes.textKo: AI로부터의 멸종 위험을 완화하는 것은 팬데믹 및 핵전쟁과 같은 다른 사회 규모 위험과 함께 글로벌 우선순위여야 한다.
quotes.context: Advocacy for treating AI safety as a civilizational imperative, not just a technical problem for researchers.
quotes.contextKo: AI 안전을 단순히 연구원의 기술적 문제가 아닌 문명적 명령으로 다루기 위한 옹호

quotes.text: The hard part of running a business is that there are a hundred things that you could be doing, and only five of those actually matter, and only one of them matters more than all of the rest of them combined.
quotes.textKo: 사업 운영의 어려운 부분은 할 수 있는 일이 백 가지이지만 그 중 오직 다섯 가지만 실제로 중요하고 그 중 하나만 나머지를 모두 합친 것보다 더 중요하다는 것이다.
quotes.context: On strategic focus and the critical importance of identifying and executing on the highest-impact opportunities in business.
quotes.contextKo: 전략적 초점과 사업에서 가장 큰 영향을 미치는 기회를 식별하고 실행하는 것의 중요성에 대해

quotes.text: Every time we stare at a new technology, we assume they're all gonna go away. It's true that some jobs go away, but we find so many new things to do and hopefully so many better things to do.
quotes.textKo: 새로운 기술을 볼 때마다 우리는 그것들이 모두 사라질 것이라고 가정한다. 일부 일자리는 사라지는 것이 사실이지만, 우리는 해야 할 새로운 일들과 바라건대 훨씬 더 나은 일들을 많이 찾는다.
quotes.context: On technological disruption and job displacement, emphasizing historical patterns of economic adaptation and new opportunity creation.
quotes.contextKo: 기술적 혼란과 일자리 이동에 대해, 경제 적응과 새로운 기회 창출의 역사적 패턴을 강조

authored.title: None identified
authored.note: Sam Altman has not authored a formal published book. However, his blog (blog.samaltman.com) and essays constitute his most significant written contributions to entrepreneurship and technology thought leadership.

recommended.title: Zero to One: Notes on Startups, or How to Build the Future
recommended.author: Peter Thiel
recommended.why: Foundational text on creating truly new ventures rather than incremental improvements. Altman recommends this to anyone who wants to innovate rather than optimize. Core philosophy influenced his thinking on OpenAI's mission.
recommended.whyKo: 점진적 개선보다는 진정한 새로운 사업 창출에 대한 기초 텍스트. Altman은 최적화가 아닌 혁신을 원하는 누구나에게 이것을 추천. 핵심 철학은 OpenAI 미션에 대한 그의 생각에 영향을 미침.

recommended.title: Man's Search for Meaning
recommended.author: Viktor Frankl
recommended.why: Explores finding meaning in life despite suffering through Frankl's Holocaust experiences. Altman values the philosophical framework for understanding purpose and resilience.
recommended.whyKo: Frankl의 홀로코스트 경험을 통해 고통에도 불구하고 인생에서 의미를 찾는 것을 탐구. Altman은 목적과 회복력을 이해하기 위한 철학적 프레임워크를 중시.

recommended.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
recommended.author: Daniel Kahneman
recommended.why: Nobel Prize-winning research on decision-making, behavioral economics, and cognitive biases. Essential for understanding why intuition is sometimes less reliable than rigorous analysis.
recommended.whyKo: 의사결정, 행동경제학, 인지 편향에 대한 노벨상 수상 연구. 직관이 때때로 엄격한 분석보다 신뢰할 수 없는 이유를 이해하는 데 필수적.

recommended.title: The Making of a Manager
recommended.author: Julie Zhuo
recommended.why: Practical guide for new managers to develop leadership skills. Altman noted that startups rarely set up managers for success and this book provides essential tools.
recommended.whyKo: 신규 관리자가 리더십 기술을 개발하기 위한 실용적인 가이드. Altman은 스타트업이 관리자를 성공하도록 설정하는 경우가 드물다고 지적했으며 이 책이 필수 도구를 제공.

recommended.title: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
recommended.author: Nick Bostrom
recommended.why: Altman considers this required reading as 'the definitive guide to what happens when machines get smarter than us.' Critical for understanding AI risks and control problems.
recommended.whyKo: Altman은 이것을 '기계가 우리보다 똑똑해지면 일어나는 일에 대한 최종 가이드'라는 필독서로 간주. AI 위험과 제어 문제를 이해하는 데 중요.

recommended.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
recommended.author: Yuval Noah Harari
recommended.why: Sweeping historical overview of human civilization and major revolutions shaping history. Provides context for understanding technology's role in human development.
recommended.whyKo: 인류 문명과 역사를 형성한 주요 혁명의 광범위한 역사 개요. 인간 발전에서 기술의 역할을 이해하기 위한 맥락을 제공.

recommended.title: Winning
recommended.author: Jack Welch
recommended.why: Practical advice from legendary CEO on creating innovative organizations and building winning teams. Insights into organizational culture and performance.
recommended.whyKo: 혁신적인 조직 창출과 승리하는 팀 구축에 대한 전설적인 CEO의 실용적인 조언. 조직 문화와 성과에 대한 통찰력.

recommended.title: The Republic
recommended.author: Plato
recommended.why: Foundational philosophical text on justice, governance, and the nature of reality. Represents Altman's interest in long-term civilizational thinking.
recommended.whyKo: 정의, 지배, 현실의 본질에 대한 기초 철학 텍스트. Altman의 장기적 문명 사고에 대한 관심을 나타냄.

personalTraits.knownFor: Revolutionary leadership in AI through OpenAI and ChatGPT's emergence as transformative technology. Visionary entrepreneurship across multiple domains: location-based services, venture capital, AI research, and nuclear energy. Exceptional ability to attract and inspire talent. Deep strategic thinking about existential risks and long-term technological impacts. Unusual combination of technical acumen, business sophistication, and philosophical depth.
personalTraits.knownForKo: OpenAI와 ChatGPT의 등장을 통한 AI의 혁명적 리더십으로 변혁적 기술로 성장. 위치 기반 서비스, 벤처 캐피털, AI 연구, 핵 에너지 등 다양한 분야의 비전 있는 기업가. 인재를 유치하고 영감을 주는 탁월한 능력. 실존적 위험과 장기적 기술 영향에 대한 깊이 있는 전략적 사고. 기술 능력, 사업 정교함, 철학적 깊이의 드문 조합.
personalTraits.philanthropy: OpenAI mission to ensure AI benefits all of humanity. Through various funds and investments, supported emerging technology companies. Interested in nuclear energy as solution to climate and energy challenges. Focus on longtermism and existential risk mitigation. Publicly advocates for AI safety research and responsible AI development.
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: AI가 모든 인류에게 이로움을 주도록 보장하는 OpenAI 미션. 다양한 펀드와 투자를 통해 신흥 기술 회사 지원. 기후 및 에너지 문제의 해결책으로 핵 에너지에 관심. 장기주의와 실존적 위험 완화에 초점. 공개적으로 AI 안전 연구와 책임 있는 AI 개발 옹호.
personalTraits.controversies: November 2023 boardroom removal from OpenAI raised questions about governance, transparency, and board dynamics. Significant wealth concentration through equity stakes in high-value companies like Reddit. Concerns about OpenAI's transition from nonprofit to for-profit structure and potential mission drift. Debates about AI safety priorities and industry influence. Scrutiny of his relationship with powerful investors and potential conflicts of interest.
personalTraits.controversiesKo: 2023년 11월 OpenAI에서의 이사회 제거는 지배 구조, 투명성, 이사회 역학에 대한 의문 제기. Reddit 같은 고가치 회사의 주식 지분을 통한 상당한 부의 집중. OpenAI의 비영리에서 영리 구조로의 전환 및 미션 변질 가능성에 대한 우려. AI 안전 우선순위 및 산업 영향에 대한 논쟁. 강력한 투자자와의 관계 및 이해 충돌의 가능성에 대한 정밀 조사.

pivotalMentors.name: Paul Graham
pivotalMentors.relationship: Mentor and Y Combinator co-founder who recruited Altman as president
pivotalMentors.influence: Graham's trust in Altman despite Loopt's failure was transformative. Mentored strategic thinking about startups and technology. Altman often cites Graham's emphasis on being 'relentlessly resourceful' as underrated advice that shaped his approach.

pivotalMentors.name: Peter Thiel
pivotalMentors.relationship: Investor, mentor, and founder of Thiel Fellowship
pivotalMentors.influence: Primary backer and financial supporter of Altman's Hydrazine Capital fund. Shaped Altman's thinking on technology, longtermism, and contrarian thinking. Through Thiel's example, learned about bold bets and unconventional career moves.

additionalInsights.dailyRoutine: Wakes between 6:00-7:00 AM. Practices 15-hour daily intermittent fasting for energy and focus. Starts day with espresso and non-reactive time: no emails or social media, instead meditation or brief exercise. First few hours are most productive and protected from meetings. Afternoon reserved for meetings, preferring 15-20 minute or 2-hour formats (avoiding default 1-hour slots). Lifts heavy weights three times weekly. Strict discipline on environment: natural light, quiet spaces, uninterrupted focus time. Second espresso shot post-lunch with approximately 200mg daily caffeine intake. Vegetarian since childhood. Sleeps in cold, dark, quiet room with Chili Pad for temperature control. Uses sleep tracker and quarterly blood tests to guide supplementation (metformin, methyl B12, omega-3, iron, vitamin D3). Relies on written paper lists for organization tracking yearly, monthly, and daily objectives.
keyAnecdotes: At age 8, received Apple Macintosh and began self-teaching programming and hardware disassembly, launching a lifelong technology passion.
At age 19, co-founded Loopt while still at Stanford, demonstrating early entrepreneurial conviction despite being in elite university.
When Steve Jobs called Loopt 'not very good,' it was a crushing blow that made him question everything but later became a learning experience about resilience.
During Y Combinator presidency (2014-2019), led the organization to fund ~1,900 companies worth collectively over $200 billion, transforming startup ecosystem.
November 2023 five-day ouster and reinstatement as OpenAI CEO: 95% of employees threatened resignation, board was entirely replaced, demonstrating exceptional employee loyalty.
Reddit stake of 12.2 million shares worth ~$1.4 billion resulted from early Y Combinator investment, showcasing long-term value creation in portfolio companies.
additionalInsights.turningPoint: Joining Y Combinator as President in 2014 marked the fundamental pivot from founder to ecosystem architect and institutional leader. Though Loopt was a relative failure, Paul Graham's faith in Altman despite the failed startup redirected his trajectory toward influence at scale. The Y Combinator role provided platform, capital, and network to shape technology industry. OpenAI leadership (2019) and ChatGPT's breakthrough (2022-2023) established him as central figure in AI revolution. November 2023 crisis and dramatic reinstatement solidified his position as indispensable to OpenAI's mission and the broader AI safety agenda.
additionalInsights.managementStyle: Visionary leadership combined with intense personal discipline and high standards. Emphases relentless focus on critical path items and ruthless deprioritization of everything else. Data-driven decision making with quantitative rigor. Open communication about risks and challenges (though November 2023 suggested board communication gaps). Attracts exceptional talent through clarity of mission and belief in company purpose. Empowering style that trusts capable individuals but with clear strategic guardrails. Known for long-term thinking that can appear counterintuitive to short-term pressures. Balances optimism about technology's potential with brutal honesty about existential risks. Collaborative in partnerships with major institutional actors (Microsoft, government) while maintaining independent voice on AI safety and policy.
